metricsSummary: Season context: SS26 week 10 of 26. Roughly 3.6 months remain in the selling season. Overall: revenue $212,964, gross margin 46%, blended sell-through 25%, inventory at cost $342,406, inventory at retail $862,975, annualized turns 1.8x. Top 10 brands by revenue: ICHI ($56,838, ST 24%, GM 63%, sold 1,023, stock 3,159); DAZE ($29,333, ST 31%, GM 56%, sold 204, stock 464); Z SUPPLY ($26,480, ST 34%, GM 56%, sold 273, stock 525); B YOUNG ($22,082, ST 21%, GM 63%, sold 481, stock 1,853); RD STYLE ($22,005, ST 26%, GM 62%, sold 348, stock 987); PILGRIM ($15,391, ST 24%, GM 59%, sold 335, stock 1,051); SAINT TROPEZ ($10,829, ST 18%, GM 60%, sold 156, stock 695); DEX ($7,590, ST 27%, GM 61%, sold 113, stock 303); DELUC ($6,634, ST 25%, GM 58%, sold 95, stock 279); SADIE AND SAGE ($3,931, ST 20%, GM 57%, sold 67, stock 273). Bottom 5 brands by sell-through: GLAMOROUS (9%, $80, stock 43); GENTLE FAWN (10%, $20, stock 9); CAMILLE (11%, $33, stock 8); SECOND SKIN (15%, $2,277, stock 370); BRUNETTE (15%, $631, stock 33). Categories: TOP (ST 21%, WOS 39.0, stock 3,798, revenue $46,737); DENIM (ST 28%, WOS 26.0, stock 731, revenue $34,210); SWEATER (ST 37%, WOS 17.0, stock 875, revenue $30,708); PANT (ST 23%, WOS 33.0, stock 1,007, revenue $22,162); JACKET (ST 39%, WOS 15.0, stock 270, revenue $20,420); JEWELRY (ST 23%, WOS 33.0, stock 949, revenue $13,766); CARDIGAN (ST 28%, WOS 25.0, stock 440, revenue $11,672); BLAZER (ST 19%, WOS 44.0, stock 258, revenue $5,711); DRESS (ST 14%, WOS 62.0, stock 579, revenue $5,571); ACCESSORIES (ST 18%, WOS 46.0, stock 640, revenue $4,599); VEST (ST 47%, WOS 11.0, stock 72, revenue $2,776); SKIRT (ST 23%, WOS 33.0, stock 177, revenue $2,383). Reorder alerts count: 7. Reorder-now styles: ICHI — CORNSTALK JACKET (0.2/wk, 100%); ICHI — BLACK MESH BALLET FLAT (0.1/wk, 100%); B YOUNG — CHERRY OVERSIZED CREWNECK SWEATER (0.0/wk, 100%); RD STYLE — SAYLOR BLOUSE (CREAM) (0.1/wk, 100%); DAZE — PLEASER HIGH RISE ANKLE JEAN (BLACK) (0.4/wk, 83%); SADIE AND SAGE — CREAM WIDE LEG DENIM (0.0/wk, 100%); SAGE THE LABEL — NAVY STRETCH TOP (0.0/wk, 100%). Top sold-out styles by velocity: ICHI CORNSTALK JACKET (0.2/wk, 100%, 6.8 weeks); RD STYLE SAYLOR BLOUSE (CREAM) (0.1/wk, 100%, 29.4 weeks); ICHI BLACK MESH BALLET FLAT (0.1/wk, 100%, 47.6 weeks); SADIE AND SAGE CREAM WIDE LEG DENIM (0.0/wk, 100%, 51.9 weeks); SAGE THE LABEL NAVY STRETCH TOP (0.0/wk, 100%, 47.6 weeks). Size gaps under-bought: M (+4 pts; demand 25%, buy 21%). Size gaps over-bought: XXL (-4 pts; demand 1%, buy 5%); XL (-3 pts; demand 7%, buy 10%); XS (-2 pts; demand 15%, buy 17%). Store breakdown: Saint John: $95,838 revenue, 41%, 1,646 units, AOV $133; Moncton: $67,883 revenue, 50%, 992 units, AOV $160; Halifax: $49,243 revenue, 47%, 748 units, AOV $122. Best performers: RD STYLE — SOFT KNIT BROWN HALF ZIP (9 units, $711); PILGRIM — GIFT WITH PURCHASE (RED HEART - GOLD) (9 units, $0); RD STYLE — DARK GREY CREW NECK PULLOVER (8 units, $316); RD STYLE — VICTORIA BLACK PULL ON SWEATS (6 units, $474); ICHI — IVO GREY BEANIE (6 units, $117); ICHI — REFINED SQUARE NECK TOP (BLACK) (6 units, $147); ICHI — REFINED SQUARE NECK TOP (CHOCOLATE) (6 units, $157); ICHI — KATE OFFICE WIDE LEG TROUSER (BLK) (6 units, $579). Slow movers: ICHI — DAHLIA SKINNY BROWN BELT (1 units, $23, 0.1/wk); PILGRIM — JAMESON SILVER RING (1 units, $59, 0.1/wk); PILGRIM — SPARKLE GOLD NECKLACE SET (1 units, $69, 0.1/wk); ICHI — DASILA CARDIGAN (BLUE) (1 units, $69, 0.1/wk); B YOUNG — FUZZY CONTRAST SWEATER (1 units, $55, 0.1/wk); Z SUPPLY — SCOUT COTTON JERSEY PANT (GREEN) (1 units, $53, 0.1/wk); Z SUPPLY — ALL DAY KNIT JACKET (CHOCOLATE) (1 units, $101, 0.1/wk); LA — TIE FRONT KNIT CARDI (CREAM) (1 units, $48, 0.1/wk). Customers: total 6,904, with orders 2,024, total spent $606,987. Top customers: Alissa DeWare ($0, 32 orders); Alison Bulmer ($0, 16 orders); Alexandra Woodworth ($0, 47 orders); Linda Boudreau ($0, 29 orders); Karen Thomas ($0, 30 orders). Channels: 30d revenue $32,432 with POS $25,576 and online $6,856.
seasonContext: SS26 is in week 10 of 26 with about 3.6 months left. It is still early enough that March-to-June acceleration matters, so decisions should focus on clean reorders in proven winners and tighter control on slow categories.

briefing.morningGreeting: Good morning — revenue is $212,964 so far this season, with ICHI leading the brand stack. Focus today on protecting winners and clearing capital from slow inventory.
briefing.healthContext: The dashboard is carrying meaningful inventory while spring sell-through is still early. That makes turns and weeks of supply look heavy now, especially in TOP, but the right move is selective reorders plus faster action on dead stock.

salesAnalytics.revenueInsight: ICHI and TOP are driving the top line right now. Push what is already converting instead of spreading buys too wide across weak brands.
salesAnalytics.brandInsight: ICHI deserves close review for reorder depth, while the lowest sell-through brands should be cut back or marked down. Keep brand decisions simple: grow proven demand, reduce stuck inventory.
salesAnalytics.channelInsight: POS remains the main engine, with online still smaller but usable for targeted sell-through pushes. Use email and Shopify to help clear long-tail product instead of discounting broad assortments in-store.

sellThrough.overallInsight: Current sell-through is early-season and should not be judged like late May. Reorder proven winners fast, but do not overreact by chasing every small sample.
sellThrough.topPerformers: ICHI CORNSTALK JACKET is already telling you where demand is real. Rebuy into proven velocity and keep urbanbutter priority brands visible when they are earning it.
sellThrough.slowMovers: Review the slowest brands and categories now, not at season end. Mark down or transfer anything with weak velocity before it becomes carryover.
sellThrough.brandRecommendations: Grow the brands with strong revenue, clean margin, and real velocity. Maintain middle performers, and reduce or clear brands sitting on too much stock for their current sell-through.

inventoryHealth.agingInsight: Aging inventory is the main risk because tied-up capital will block opportunistic reorders. Pull a clean list of 60+ day product and decide this week what gets marked down, transferred, or held.
inventoryHealth.categoryWOS: Watch weeks of supply closely in categories with weak sell-through. Keep future receipts focused on the categories already proving demand.
inventoryHealth.sizeGaps: Use size data to fix buy shape, not just total units. Add depth where demand is outrunning buys and trim sizes that keep stacking up.

buyPlanning.otbContext: Open-to-buy should stay focused on categories and brands already earning their way back in. With 3.6 months left, the job is to stay flexible enough to reorder winners without deepening weak inventory.
buyPlanning.seasonalComparison: SS25 was a 2-store base and SS26 is now 3 stores, so raw volume is not a clean comparison on its own. Judge this season by sell-through quality, margin, and whether winners are scaling cleanly store to store.
buyPlanning.actionItems: 1) Reorder the top proven winners, especially any sold-out styles with real weekly velocity. 2) Build a markdown list for the slowest brands and 60+ day inventory. 3) Adjust future receipts toward the sizes and categories already showing demand.

customers.vipInsight: Top customers are worth protecting with early access to new winners and personal follow-up. Keep your best spenders close to the strongest new deliveries.
customers.segmentInsight: Customer mix should guide how aggressive you are with reorders versus clearance. Lean on loyal repeat shoppers to move high-conviction product first.
